Power consumers in india can save ₹2.5 trn by doublAC efficiency 

a new study has found the country can avoid power shortages and save consumers up to ~2.5 trillion by doubling the energy eiciency of room air conditioners (ACs) over the next decade. 

India added 10-15 million new AC annually , with another 130 -150 million expected overall the next decade 

road map could reduce peak demand by 10 Gw by 2030 and 47 Gw by 2035 equivalent to roughly 100 large power plants saving an estimated ~8 trillion ($80 billion) in avoided power infrastructure investment.

Compressed Natural Gas (CNG) prices in Delhi have been hiked by Rs 2 per kg, effective 6 AM on Tuesday, May 26, marking a fresh increase in fuel costs for consumers.

Following the hike, CNG will cost Rs 83.09 per kg in Delhi.

Over the last to weeks, CNG prices have been increased four times as the city gas distributors continue to grapple with mounting losses due to the Iran war, which has choked global energy supply chains.

📊 Poly Medicure FY26 Highlights  
Acquisitions, Dividend & Expansion Focus | MCap ₹12,768 Cr

📌 FY26 Results & Dividend

- Poly Medicure approved audited standalone and consolidated results for Q4 and FY ended March 31, 2026.

- Recommended dividend of ₹3.5 per equity share (70%) for FY26, subject to shareholder approval.

📌 Strategic Expansion

- Acquired Pendracare Group and Medistream SA, strengthening global presence and product portfolio.

- Acquisitions impacted goodwill and FY26 financial statements.

📌 Operational Developments

- Recognized impact of upcoming new Labour Codes under Exceptional Items for FY26.

- Allocated 33,775 ESOP shares at ₹100 per share during FY26.

- Utilized QIP proceeds for:
  
  - Capex expansion
  
  - Inorganic growth initiatives
  
  - Temporary investments

📌 Governance & Audit

- Auditors issued an unmodified opinion on standalone and consolidated financial statements.

📌 Market Snapshot

- Current Market Capitalisation stands at approximately ₹12,768 crore.

💰 Denta Water Delivers Strong FY26 Performance  
₹7,278M Order Book | Healthy Profitability | Positioned to Benefit from Jal Jeevan Mission | MCap ₹711 Cr

📌 Order Book & Growth Visibility

- Outstanding order book stood at ₹7,277.76 million as of March 31, 2026, providing strong revenue visibility.

- Management expects continued business momentum driven by opportunities under the Jal Jeevan Mission.

📌 FY26 Financial Performance

- Revenue from Operations for FY26 came in at ₹2,032.85 million on both consolidated and standalone basis.

- Profit Before Tax (PBT) stood at ₹820.59 million (Consolidated) and ₹819.26 million (Standalone).

- Profit After Tax (PAT) reported at ₹528.85 million (Consolidated) and ₹608.44 million (Standalone).

- Basic & Diluted EPS stood at ₹22.81 (Consolidated) and ₹22.79 (Standalone).

📌 Operational Highlights

- Employee headcount increased to 79 employees, reflecting approximately 23% YoY operational growth.

- Company achieved nearly 15% YoY growth in profit volumes during FY26.

- ERP system implementation is currently underway to improve operational efficiency and strengthen execution capabilities.

📌 Growth Drivers & Outlook

- Utilisation of IPO proceeds supported business expansion and execution growth during the year.

- Government-led Jal Jeevan Mission is expected to create significant long-term opportunities for the company.

📌 Market Snapshot

- Current Market Capitalisation stands at approximately ₹711 crore.

🏥 Yatharth Hospital Accelerates Expansion Journey  
Q4 Revenue Surges 47% YoY | Targets ~5,000 Beds Over Next 3 Years | MCap ₹6,718 Cr

📌 Expansion & Capacity Growth

- Total operational bed capacity expanded to 2,555 beds, excluding the under-construction 250-bed Gurugram hospital.

- Company plans to scale capacity to nearly 5,000 beds within the next 3 years, strengthening its presence across North India.

- Newly launched hospitals in Greater Faridabad, Faridabad Sector-20, New Delhi, and Agra contributed ₹753 million, accounting for 22% of Group revenues in Q4 FY26.

- Yatharth acquired a 250-bed under-construction hospital in Gurugram for ₹100 crore, expected to become operational by April 2027.

📌 Financial Performance

- Revenue from Operations grew 47% YoY to ₹3,416 million in Q4 FY26.

- FY26 Revenue from Operations increased 36% YoY to ₹12,072 million.

- Q4 EBITDA stood at ₹799 million, up 37% YoY, with EBITDA Margin at 23.4%.

- FY26 EBITDA came in at ₹2,921 million, registering 30% YoY growth, with EBITDA Margin at 24.2%.

- Excluding new hospitals, the company reported a strong Adjusted EBITDA Margin of 30.4% in Q4 FY26 and 28.5% for FY26.

- Q4 PAT rose 15% YoY to ₹447 million, with PAT Margin at 13.1%.

- FY26 PAT increased 30% YoY to ₹1,703 million, with PAT Margin at 14.1%.

📌 Operational Highlights

- The newly commissioned Agra hospital achieved monthly revenues of nearly ₹7 crore and delivered Q4 EBITDA margin above 15%.

- Successfully performed Agra’s first Da Vinci Robotic Surgeries, marking a major technological milestone.

- Yatharth Hospital has become the exclusive clinical partner to Noida International Airport for Medical Value Travel (MVT) initiatives.

📌 Shareholder Value Creation

- The company has delivered an impressive 188% shareholder return since listing.

- Current Market Capitalisation stands at approximately ₹6,718 crore.

text: NLC India Signs MoU with NPCIL for Development of Nuclear Power Projects through a JV Company

Indian EV purchases are increasingly being driven by arithmetic, not aspiration.🧮

The Indian EV transition is increasingly being pulled by economics, not only pushed by policy. A cumulative ₹5/litre rise in petrol and diesel in May has made running-cost savings far more tangible for retail customers and fleets.

The illustrative math in the ₹11–12 lakh segment: EVs cost more upfront, but their 5-year running cost is far lower than petrol, diesel, or CNG. That is why EVs are increasingly being treated as a financial hedge, in addition to being a green choice. 

The market is already responding. EV PV retail share rose to 5.77% in April 2026 from 3.70% a year earlier.

EVs may still face friction on model choice, charging convenience, and resale value, to be addressed. 

The implication for industry is significant: product positioning, financing, and resale support will matter as much as battery specs. In a market like India, ownership economics will decide scale.

GPIL 

Approved setting up of a **20 GWh BESS project** and signed long-term agreements with **EVE Power** for Grade-1 **628 Ah LFP cells** and with **Shanghai Shenyi Roche Energy Technology Ltd.** for BESS Balance of System supply, thereby securing the supply chain for the project. The company also announced an investment of **INR 200 crore** for setting up **GPIL BESS**.

Key Financial Highlights H2 FY26
- Total Income of ₹ 681.00 Cr, YoY growth of 87.79%

• 📍 Partnership aimed at enhancing production from ONGC’s Western Offshore fields (excluding Mumbai High)

• ⚙️ TSP to review field performance & identify reservoir, facility and well improvement opportunities

• 🛢️ Mumbai Offshore Basin contributes around 75% of India’s domestic crude oil & natural gas production

• 📈 BP estimates potential increase of around 10.8% in crude oil production

• ⛽ Gas production potential seen rising around 31.5%

• 🔋 Overall oil equivalent production increase estimated at around 24.1%

• 📅 Incremental production benefits expected to become visible from FY27, with full-scale visibility by FY30

• 🌍 Initiative aimed at leveraging global technology expertise & operational best practices

• 🏗️ Contract structured with fixed fee initially, followed by revenue-linked service fee model

🟢 Impact: Positive — Strategic collaboration with BP could improve recovery from mature offshore assets and support ONGC’s long-term domestic production growth.

Here’s why GAIL jumped 5% despite mixed Q4 & FY26 results 👇

Q4 FY26 Highlights
• Revenue from Operations: ₹35,705 Cr (↓ 2.3% YoY | ↑ 1.1% QoQ)
• PAT: ₹1,481 Cr (↓ 40.9% YoY | ↓ 14.3% QoQ)

FY26 Highlights
• Revenue from Operations: ₹1,42,094 Cr (↓ 0.2% YoY)
• EBITDA: ₹14,524 Cr (↓ 30% YoY)
• PAT: ₹7,581 Cr (↓ 42% YoY)
• Debt-to-Equity Ratio: 0.28x

Operational Metrics
• Natural Gas Transmission: 122.18 MMSCMD (↓ 3.8% YoY)
• Natural Gas Marketing Volume: 104.21 MMSCMD (↑ 3.2% YoY)
• LPG Transmission: 4.58 MMTPA (↑ 2.7% YoY)- Highest Ever
• Petrochemical Sales: 785 TMT (↓ 7.1% YoY)

Key Business Updates
• 18,000+ km gas pipeline network with 65% market share
• LNG portfolio at 16.56 MMTPA
• Plans to source an additional 4–5 MMTPA LNG by 2030
• Renewable portfolio expansion from 155 MW to 1,063 MW in progress

🔍 Analysis
Despite pressure on profitability due to weaker gas margins and global energy volatility, GAIL’s core transmission and marketing businesses remained resilient. The company continues to strengthen India’s gas infrastructure with pipeline expansion, LNG sourcing, petrochemical capacity additions, and renewable energy investments. Operationally, gas marketing volumes and LPG transmission remained strong, showing healthy domestic demand trends. The long-term India gasification theme, LNG expansion, and clean-energy transition continue to remain key structural growth drivers for GAIL. The optimism in the stock came as crude oil prices declined amid reports of progress in US-Iran negotiations, which have revived hopes of the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

Park Medi World accelerates growth with acquisition of Medicity Hospital, Rudrapur.

Park Medi World Limited has announced acquisition of The Medicity Hospital in Rudrapur, Uttarakhand, marking a significant step in its expansion journey. Through this transaction, the company is acquiring V3 Healthcare Private Limited, which owns and operates the hospital, in a deal valued at approximately ₹177 Cr.

The Medicity Hospital is a well-established multi-super speciality facility with a capacity of around 330 beds and is one of the largest healthcare institutions in the Kumaon region. It offers a wide range of advanced medical services across more than 20 specialties and is supported by modern infrastructure and a strong clinical team.

This acquisition marks Park Medi’s entry into Uttarakhand and strengthens its presence across North India. The move aligns with the company’s strategy of expanding into high-potential regions and building a wider, integrated healthcare network. By adding a large and established hospital to its portfolio, the company aims to enhance service offerings, improve operational efficiency, and drive long-term growth.

The transaction will be completed in phases, with a majority stake to be acquired in the near term and the remaining stake over the long term. Overall, this acquisition is expected to strengthen the company’s footprint, improve its scale, and support sustained value creation going forward.

- Running plants at low load increases wear & tear

- BESS tariffs fell below ₹3/unit with record ₹2.70/kWh discovery
- Battery storage emerging as stronger balancing solution
- Coal flexibility increasingly becoming economic challenge

Policy Update

- Comparative assessment underway between flexible coal and BESS economics

text:
JPM on LTM
Downgrade to N, TP cut to Rs 4500 from Rs 5100

LTM announced that it has issued an offer to acquire Randstad’s technology and consulting services business in Europe and Australia.

Revenues of these entities (€469m) have declined sharply in the past two years while estimate business operates at much lower 4-5% Ebitda margins, given its onshore-heavy nature, which should be materially margin-dilutive.

LTM has also announced concomitant contracts to take over and build a GCC for Randstad in India and a reverse contract to Randstad to support LTM’s subcon needs
While, across three contracts, management doesn’t see any EPS dilution, estimate acquisition alone could drive 2% EPS dilution due to amortization costs and lower interest income.

Moreover, don’t see obvious horizontal service line or vertical synergies, given its focus on digital engineering and cybersecurity rather than core IT Services, which LTM focuses on.
Cross-sell opportunities as they exist should be limited by different buying centers for engineering and IT services

Acquisition price appears to be modest at €160m on a EV/Sales basis (0.34x), but not on implied profit multiples (8x EBITDA, 13-15x EBIT), given global/EU peer multiples.

More than price paid, see this acquisition as a sign that organic growth opportunities are drying up and management is being forced to evaluate unusual acquisitions to bulk up rather than thoughtfully add capabilities. Fear management distraction, due to closure of the acquisition by 3Q27 and ekeing out of synergies after that, could weigh on execution.

Moderate organic revenues/EPS by 1-2%, cut target PE multiple from 21x to 19x
channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

CHEMICAL SECTOR : CRUDE OIL SPIKE TRIGGERS MARGIN PRESSURE - ET

• 🧪 Key raw materials like benzene, phenol, propylene, styrene & ethylene surged 16–80% from pre-war levels

• 📉 Analysts expect EBITDA margins of chemical companies to decline by 100–600 bps in FY27

• 🏭 Commodity chemical players likely to face higher pressure due to crude-linked inputs & Chinese oversupply

• ⚡ Specialty chemical companies relatively better placed due to stronger customer stickiness & value-based pricing

• 🌍 Supply chain disruptions, higher insurance costs & geopolitical tensions adding further cost pressures

• 📊 Despite near-term challenges, Nifty Chemicals index remained resilient and outperformed Nifty 50 in 2026

• 🔍 Companies with strong pricing power, backward integration & diversified sourcing expected to outperform

🟠 Impact: Mixed (Margin pressure likely for commodity chemical players, while specialty chemical companies may remain relatively resilient)

PRIME CABLES – FY26 BUSINESS HIGHLIGHTS  

Financial Performance
- FY26 revenue rose 67% YoY to ₹234.9 Cr
- EBITDA increased 61% YoY to ₹23.5 Cr
- PAT grew 67% YoY to ₹12.3 Cr
- H2FY26 revenue surged 70% YoY to ₹144.2 Cr

- Revenue driven mainly through EPC and tender channels

Order Book & Growth

- Strong demand from T&D and electrification projects
- Expansion into renewable and MV cable segments underway

Margin & Cash Flow

- Working capital intensity increased with higher receivables
- Growth funded through debt, equity and external financing

Key Strengths
- NABL-certified testing lab and PSU approvals
- Strong EPC relationships with high repeat business
- Focused institutional B2B strategy avoiding retail competition

Key Risks
- Working capital pressure

JPM’s Z-score model shows momentum surged to +3.90, then crashed to -5.30. Expect momentum to stay dead for 1 month. Smart move? Rotate money into classic Value stocks.

Regional banks are the ultimate hedge right now. They mix cheap Value with steady Momentum. Plus, they win big as the BOJ stays hawkish and hikes interest rates from June.

🎯 JPM Summary: Don't panic dump. Tech is just cooling down. Lock your capital into Japanese regional banks. They offer zero pain trades and clear profits from rising interest rates.

Life Insurance Corporation of India: BUY | TP 1,030 | Strong overall execution, EV impacted by MTM loss 

ITC: BUY | TP 399 | Premiumisation the key

Emami: BUY | TP 617 | Summer portfolio to aid growth in 1HFY27, Maintain BUY 

LMW: HOLD | TP 13,595 | In-line revenue; margin-led beat on profitability Shanghai Composite

Sansera Engineering: BUY | TP 3,180 | ADS & OEM outsourcing opportunity to drive long-term growth

Honasa Consumer: BUY | TP 462 | +Margin expansion an edge

JK Lakshmi Cement: HOLD | TP 680 | Leverage to rise amidst weak profitability

Le Travenues Technology: HOLD | TP 180 | Subdued quarter; near-term headwinds persist as hotels remain a long-term catalyst

Hohn’s High-Conviction Alpha Trap

Sir Chris Hohn runs a hyper-concentrated $21B book with roughly $14B parked in a single bet on GE Aerospace, weaponizing a multi-decade moat where zero new engine competitors have entered in 50 years.

Retail loves the "charitable billionaire" narrative, but Hohn’s extreme concentration introduces massive systemic tail risk. A single operational drawdown or aerospace de-rating would completely vaporize the fund's capital.

This regime shift highlights a structural corporate margin play: dominant global duopolies can easily pass through inflation-fueled input costs, squeezing labor stats while capturing immense, unchecked pricing power.

Bottom Line: Forget diversification. True alpha in this era is about finding structural monopolies with bulletproof unit economics that can survive any macro liquidity trap.

Interesting Chart - Capex Required to Integrate in Solar Chain goes up significanlty

Waaree’s biggest advantage in this solar cycle may not just be demand.

It is the CAPEX barrier created by deeper backward integration.

The numbers explain it clearly:

Module-only capacity needs ~₹100–150 Cr/GW.
Cell + module integration needs ~₹500–600 Cr/GW.
Ingot + wafer + cell + module needs ~₹1,100–1,200 Cr/GW.
Full poly-to-module integration needs ~₹1,500–1,600 Cr/GW.

That is nearly a 10–17x jump in capital intensity as the value chain goes deeper.
This changes the competitive landscape.
On the supply side, fewer players can afford this scale, capital, technology, and execution capability.
On the demand side, ALMM and domestic sourcing requirements can further shift opportunities toward integrated domestic players.

So Waaree gets a dual-cycle benefit:

Competition reduces as integration becomes capital-heavy

Demand expands for approved, integrated domestic manufacturers

This is why Waaree is not just participating in the solar upcycle.

It is structurally positioned to remain one of the front-runners.

So what everybody sees as an oversupply or competitive issue may not be true

Kalyan Capitals Ltd (KCL) – A Quiet Turnaround Story? 

What does the company do?

KCL is registered as a Non-Systemically Important Non-Deposit Taking Base Layer NBFC-ICC.

The company primarily provides funded facilities to individuals and corporates. Now, it has also entered the gold loan business — a segment seeing strong traction across India due to rising gold prices and demand for secured retail lending.

Business Momentum Improving
FY25 loan disbursement stood at ₹203.2 Cr, up ~24% YoY
Net NPA ratio remained around 3%

Valuation Looks Interesting Despite improvement in business activity:

Market Cap: ₹48.4 Cr
Enterprise Value: ₹304 Cr
P/B Ratio: 0.92x
Stock P/E: 12.6x
EV/EBITDA: 10.2x
Current Price: ₹9.21
Book Value: ₹10

The stock currently trades below book value and at a valuation lower than many listed NBFC peers.

Growth Metrics
5-Year Revenue CAGR: 39%

CLSA on Honasa

O-P, TP Rs 434

reported revenue growth of 23% YoY (28% growth ex-change in accounting) 4%/6% ahead of our estimate/consensus. 

Volume grew 30% while its margin expanded more than 650bps YoY, leading to a beat on Ebitda of 19%/24% versus our estimate/consensus. 

Three things stood out for us. 

Fisrst, Mamaearth grew in the teens YoY and management expects double-digit growth to continue. 

Second, offtake growth in general trade and modern trade was up 30% YoY, a healthy sign of rising brand traction. 

Third, operating leverage, albeit on a soft base, helped Ebitda beat our estimate by more than 140bps. 

We think Honasa’s runway to scale brands via its focus categories and hero SKUs remains long. 

Execution of the Mamaearth turnaround continues to improve and we expect margin expansion to continue.

CLSA on Nykaa

O-P TP Rs 338

reported 28% revenue growth in 4QFY26 and its highest-ever Ebitda margin of 8.4%, up 195bps YoY, with Ebitda 8% above consensus. 

Beauty NSV grew 29% YoY with margin-accretive own brands up 54%, driving a +70bps Ebitda margin. 

Fashion saw 42% YoY NSV growth with Ebitda breakeven. 

Ebit missed our estimate due to higher-than-expected depreciation. 

We lower our FY27-28CL earnings estimates by 1%-2% on account of higher depreciation, but raise our target price from Rs328 to Rs338 to reflect faster sales growth. 

We expect Ebitda margin expansion to continue, driven by rising salience of own brands and operating leverage.

Bernstein on LIC 

Market Perform, TP Rs 900

Q4 saw healthy growth in top-line for LIC.

New sales grew +22%/+18% YoY in Q4/FY26 driven by strong non-par sales

Insurer saw continued margin uplift in FY26 aided by product mix shift (non-par) and favorable yield curve movements.

CITI on LIC

Buy,  TP Rs 1475

4Q operational performance was strong, led by ~690 bps YoY expansion in VNB margin (~600 bps over CitiE) aided by higher non-par mix (YoY) and favourable yield curve led benefits in the swiftly growing non-par book.

EV compressed 3% over Sep’25 (+2% over Mar’25), largely led by elevated negative investment variance (almost similar impact between debt and equity for FY2026).

Management highlighted that the drop in net gain on FV change, as of Mar ’26, has largely (around 80%) been recouped till mid-May’26 providing comfort on EV recoup.

Management highlighted concerted efforts to sustainably drive improvement in persistency, product innovation, increase productivity of exiting agents, agent base augmentation, and higher business through non-agency.

Valuation remains benign with FY2027E projected core EV (ex-MTM EV) at levels higher than current market value.

Visibility on promoter holding structure is a key overhang even as operational performance remains sound.

CITI on ITC

Sell, TP Rs 290

4Q performance is not directly comparable with prior periods given mid-quarter changes in cigarette taxation

Estimate ~7% volume decline, driving the 24% drop in cigarette net revenues, reflecting: 

1) strong January demand; 

2) trade inventory stocking ahead of price hikes; and 

3) demand slowdown thereafter following staggered price increases to offset higher taxes.

Reported cigarette EBIT growth of ~7% is also not comparable, as it appears supported by one-off inventory gains on pre-tax revision stock

Importantly, key structural uncertainties persist, particularly around price elasticity, demand sensitivity to successive price hikes, and potential downtrading to illicit products

CLSA on ITC

O-P, TP Rs 394

Cigarettes Ebit grew 7.2% YoY in 4QFY26 and was 15% ahead of consensus.

Cigarettes net revenue for the year grew 8.2% YoY. 

FMCG revenue other than cigarettes grew 15% YoY and was 2%/5% above our estimate/consensus.
